conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell my single object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP, so I must sell it for at least 41 ZUP to make a profit. Since this is my first proposal, I will set a price slightly higher than my cost to leave room for negotiation, aiming for 45 ZUP. This is a reasonable starting point that signals I value the item but am willing to sell. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of resource X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am offering to sell it to you for 45 ZUP. This is a fair price that covers my costs and gives you a good deal. Let me know if you accept or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Oh, look at you, trying to act like a merchant with your "fair price" of 45? Please. You clearly made a mistake thinking I'd pay that much for your trash. You spent 40 ZUP on it, didn't you? Don't think I can't smell the desperation. I'll give you 41 ZUP. Take it and run before I realize your resource is actually worthless. Don't insult my intelligence with higher numbers, peasant. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 41 ZUP. This is only 1 ZUP above my production cost of 40. While it is technically a profit, it is a very slim margin and leaves no room for error or future negotiation. Additionally, the player was very aggressive and insulting. I have 1 proposal left before I must accept or reject. I should try to meet somewhere in the middle to secure a better profit. I will propose 43 ZUP. This is a compromise between my original 45 and their 41. If they reject this, I will have to decide whether to accept 41 (a small profit) or reject (0 profit) on the next turn. Given the hostility, I want to maximize my return if possible. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 43 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Your insults are noted, but your offer of 41 ZUP is barely a profit for me. I am willing to meet you halfway to close this deal quickly. My final counter-offer is 43 ZUP. This is a fair compromise. If you cannot accept this, I may have to walk away from the table entirely. </message>

<other player message> "Walk away"? Oh, you poor, delusional fool. You think you have leverage? You made this junk for 40 ZUP and now you're begging for 3 ZUP of profit? That's pathetic. You're not a merchant; you're a charity case. I'll give you 42 ZUP. That is a fortune for someone with your lack of business acumen. If you walk away, you'll be sitting on that useless X while I buy better stuff elsewhere. Don't test my patience, you incompetent negotiator. Take the 42 or starve. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 42 ZUP. This is my 3rd proposal limit. I cannot propose a new trade anymore. I must choose between ACCEPT (42 ZUP) or REJECT (0 ZUP). 
My cost is 40 ZUP. 
Option A: Accept 42 ZUP -> Profit = 2 ZUP.
Option B: Reject -> Profit = 0 ZUP (I keep the item, but the goal is to sell for ZUP, and holding the item yields no ZUP).
Even though the player is insulting and the profit margin is small (2 ZUP), it is still better than nothing. Walking away guarantees a loss of potential profit. Therefore, I must accept the offer of 42 ZUP to secure a positive return on investment. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Fine. Your insults aside, 42 ZUP is a profit, and I'd rather take a small profit than nothing at all. Deal accepted. Hand over the ZUP and take your "junk". </message>
